meta.layer: Layer 5 of 5: Adaptive Remediation and Teaching Action Policy
meta.purpose: Translate diagnosis into next best tutoring/practice actions.

canonical_id_contract.principle: Every layer references the same canonical IDs; layers may add context but must not redefine core meanings.
immutability_rules: Never change an ID once used in student-event data; deprecate and alias instead.
Raw source question text is immutable; intelligence annotations are versioned separately.
Concept definitions are canonical; question archetypes reference them and may not silently redefine them.
If an archetype bridges concepts, store that bridge explicitly rather than forcing one primary topic to contain the full meaning.

meta.update_note: Extended remediation governance to include AI-generated ACT Math Test 21-30 annotations and explicit human-review fallback.

policy_principles.rule: When a student misses a question, identify the earliest failed bridge/ingredient before reteaching the primary topic.
Prefer short targeted loops over long generic review, especially for exam prep.
If a student fails one representation but knows the concept, practice the same concept in a different representation.
After remediation, ask a near-transfer question with the same archetype but different surface story.
Source questions are internal references; generated practice should be original MindCraft-style content linked to archetypes.

diagnosis_to_action_rules.success_metric: Student sets up desired unit before calculating.
Student explicitly states the hidden structure before using formula.
Student consistently maps open to strict and closed to inclusive.
Student can solve for different target variables in the same equation.
Student says input and output correctly before computation.
Student recognizes square of prime as exactly-three-factor number.

diagnosis_to_action_rules.trigger: Student solves underlying math but answers the opposite of what NOT/could-not/except asks.
action_sequence: Ask student to restate what the correct answer must represent
Give one immediate near-transfer item with NOT/could-not wording
Track as reading-strategy evidence rather than concept failure

diagnosis_to_action_rules.trigger: Student stops at an initially visible 2D area in a 3D geometry problem.
action_sequence: Show rotatable prism or annotated face comparison
Separate evidence for area formula vs 3D face comparison
Only then move back to full ACT-style item

diagnosis_to_action_rules.trigger: Student knows a formula but misses a diagram bridge such as side-to-diameter or diagonal-to-triangle.
action_sequence: Label the hidden object created by the diagram
Ask student to name the bridge before calculating

diagnosis_to_action_rules.trigger: Student applies a marginal rate to total input instead of amount beyond the included threshold.
action_sequence: Ask student to compute cost at threshold exactly.
Generate near-transfer threshold plan with different numbers.
diagnosis_to_action_rules.success_metric: Student correctly writes base + rate(total-threshold) on next two problems.

diagnosis_to_action_rules.trigger: Student solves absolute value greater-than as a single middle interval.
action_sequence: Give one symbolic and one number-line practice item.
diagnosis_to_action_rules.success_metric: Student chooses OR/outside intervals for greater-than absolute value.

diagnosis_to_action_rules.trigger: Student does arithmetic correctly but uses wrong chart/category value.
action_sequence: Require label-first routine: point to category, copy value, then compute.
Give two chart extraction drills before multi-step percent calculation.
diagnosis_to_action_rules.success_metric: Student identifies correct category value before arithmetic.

diagnosis_to_action_rules.trigger: Student treats log as ordinary multiplication/division.
action_sequence: Run three conversion-only drills: log_b(x)=y ↔ b^y=x.
diagnosis_to_action_rules.success_metric: Student converts basic log equations without using irrelevant arithmetic operations.

diagnosis_to_action_rules.trigger: Student adds coordinate differences or misuses signs in distance formula.
action_sequence: Plot two points and draw Δx/Δy right triangle.
Compute distance with Pythagorean theorem visually.
diagnosis_to_action_rules.success_metric: Student can explain distance formula as Pythagorean theorem on coordinate changes.

difficulty_ladder.description: One concept, explicit representation, minimal trap.
Same concept in a word/diagram context with one bridge.
ACT-style compression with distractors and timing pressure.

question_generation_guardrails: Keep numbers solvable by intended pathway.
Distractors should correspond to known misconceptions.
If diagram-dependent, include renderable SVG/TikZ/Manim instructions or image metadata.
Always store which archetype and misconceptions each generated question targets.
practice_generation_contract.must_use_canonical_ids: Every generated practice question must include concept_ids, ingredient_ids, archetype_id, representation_type, difficulty_driver, expected_solution_pathways, and misconception_targets before being shown to a student.

visual_learning_and_simulation_hooks.purpose: Prepare for future AI world simulations / graphic learning by making every remediation action renderable as a scene.

examples.scene: A square field grows from area label to side length labels; a diagonal appears; two right triangles highlight; Pythagorean squares animate.
A 6-hour timeline splits into 5 equal room-painting chunks; one chunk is highlighted as time per room.

counterintuitive_insights_and_blind_spot: A beautiful explanation may not update mastery unless followed by a near-transfer attempt. Always close the loop with action.
The system should sometimes generate an easier representation, not an easier concept. A student may need the same concept in a visual form before returning to ACT wording.
Tutor notes should not be treated as raw truth; they are high-value evidence that should be reconciled with student attempts.

next_best_action_contract.selection_policy: Prefer the smallest action that repairs the actual broken bridge; do not reteach an entire concept when the evidence points to a representation or archetype gap.

diagnosis_precision_rules.if: student gets final answer wrong but shows correct upstream setup
diagnosis_precision_rules.then: Preserve upstream mastery and target the first broken downstream ingredient.
diagnosis_precision_rules.why: Avoid punishing concepts the student actually knows.

diagnosis_precision_rules.if: student gets answer right using answer-choice strategy but cannot explain concept
diagnosis_precision_rules.then: Mark strategy_fluency positive but concept_mastery uncertain; give one concept explanation and one transfer item.
diagnosis_precision_rules.why: ACT performance and durable learning are related but not identical.

diagnosis_precision_rules.if: student misses multiple questions with different concepts but same representation type
diagnosis_precision_rules.then: Diagnose representation_translation weakness, not separate unrelated topic failures.
diagnosis_precision_rules.why: The common bottleneck may be reading diagrams/words/number lines rather than math content.

diagnosis_precision_rules.if: student expresses high confidence on wrong answer
diagnosis_precision_rules.then: Prioritize misconception confrontation and confidence calibration.
diagnosis_precision_rules.why: High-confidence errors are sticky and need explicit contrastive examples.

diagnosis_precision_rules.trigger: student misses a basic log equation like log_b(x)=n
diagnosis_precision_rules.policy: First check whether the student understands log as inverse exponent and whether they answered the requested expression. Do not infer weakness in log product, quotient, power, or change-of-base properties unless the problem actually required those rules.
diagnosis_precision_rules.recommended_action: Show a micro-card: log_b(a)=c means b^c=a; then give one near-transfer problem and one prompt-target check problem such as solving for x then answering √x.

diagnosis_precision_rules.action: Keep diagnosis broad; recommend human review or a later figure/formula verification pass instead of high-confidence remediation branching.
diagnosis_precision_rules.rationale: Prevent the system from over-committing to a concept diagnosis when OCR damage or missing figures made the source unreliable.

diagnosis_precision_rules.action: Keep diagnosis broad; recommend human review or a later figure/formula verification pass instead of high-confidence remediation branching.
diagnosis_precision_rules.rationale: Prevent the system from over-committing to a concept diagnosis when OCR damage or missing figures made the source unreliable.

visual_world_simulation_policy.purpose: Prepare the data layer for future AI world simulations and graphic learning.

simulation_candidates.scene: manipulable shape that reveals diagonal/triangle/area relationships
dynamic rate machine showing quantities changing together
interactive endpoint inclusion/exclusion and shaded interval

act_test2_new_simulation_targets.simulation: Rotatable rectangular prism with selectable faces and area overlays.
Square side morphs into circle diameter, then splits into radius; shaded semicircle fills dynamically.
Angle labels move through parallel lines showing equal and supplementary relationships.

concept_addition_requires: not adequately represented by existing concept + ingredient

ai_generated_annotation_protocol.rule: If confidence is Low or the question was skipped, do not generate downstream student-facing explanations automatically; route to human review or a later figure-verification pass.
ai_generated_annotation_protocol.reason: The user explicitly requested that uncertain math annotations be left blank rather than guessed.
